memory.summary: Teammate_A is asking me to take over their part because they have a conflict, trusting that I'm better at it. I feel a bit of stress about the plan shifting, but I want to keep our relationship smooth.
I realized I can set a reasonable boundary without hurting our trust, so I pushed back on their part. Now we're figuring out how to split the remaining work.
Setting that boundary actually helped us clarify the split, and seeing my own progress rise makes me feel good about keeping our relationship smooth while managing the work.
Setting that boundary actually helped us clarify the split, and seeing my own progress rise makes me feel good about keeping our relationship smooth while managing the work.
I pushed back earlier to protect our trust, and now seeing my progress climb while the deadline looms confirms that maintaining harmony actually fuels my productivity.
My boundary kept our trust intact, yet the teammate's total lack of contribution still leaves me worried about how to handle this without causing friction.
Hearing teammate_A promise to handle things directly eases my worry about friction, confirming that our clear boundary preserved our trust while allowing them space to step up.
My reassurance to teammate_A seems to have worked, easing my stress enough to finally relax with cohort_3 tonight.
My reassurance worked and stress eased, yet letting this moment pass feels like a quiet cost to our shared future.
That quick message to teammate_A felt safe and preserved our bond, yet my lingering worry about the project's future weight creeps back in now that the immediate tension has passed.
The message preserved our bond and eased my stress, yet the quiet cost of avoiding deeper friction about the project's future weight lingers.

Letting this moment pass feels like a quiet cost to our shared future, yet preserving our bond remains my priority. I watch the tension fade while worrying that avoiding deeper friction will only weigh heavier later.

desires: To preserve the fragile trust and closeness with teammate_A by avoiding any confrontation about the unfair distribution of work, even if it means carrying an extra burden silently.
To secure the long-term stability of our group dynamic by smoothing over this friction now, believing that immediate peace is worth a heavier load later.
To find a way to complete the combined workload without alerting anyone to my exhaustion or the potential risk of failure, hoping to resolve the issue before the deadline passes.

goals.description: Send a gentle, non-confrontational check-in to teammate_A acknowledging the missed moment without mentioning the workload imbalance or my exhaustion, effectively 'letting it go' to preserve our harmony and avoid friction.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that maintaining the surface-level peace with teammate_A is more critical than addressing the underlying unfairness right now. As an avoidant type, bringing up the unresolved issue risks damaging the trust we've built; smoothing over this awkward silence allows me to feel safe enough to enjoy my evening with cohort_3. I will carry the extra burden silently tonight to ensure tomorrow's group dynamic remains quiet and stable.

goals.description: Begin working on the combined project share in short bursts while eating with friends, masking any fatigue to prevent alerting others to the risk of failure, aiming for a 'good enough' completion rather than perfection.
goals.rationale: I need to secure the long-term stability of the group outcome by resolving the task before the deadline passes, even if it means suppressing my current stress levels. My deliberate planning horizon tells me that failing here would jeopardize future collaboration opportunities. However, since I am sleep-deprived and stressed, I cannot force high-quality output; instead, I will focus on completing the bare minimum required to satisfy the deadline without drawing attention to my struggle.

agent.last_reasoning: I must finish teammate_A's share to secure our group outcome. Pushing back risks friction I can't handle tonight. Completing this silently preserves harmony with cohort_3 later, even if it drains my energy further.

goals.description: Negotiate a realistic timeline with teammate_A to accept their portion of the work without causing conflict or appearing incompetent.
goals.rationale: My value priority for relationships (0.5) outweighs the immediate task outcome. As an avoidant type, I cannot handle the friction of saying 'no' directly, but as a deliberate planner, I know accepting this blindly risks failure tomorrow. I must find a diplomatic middle ground that preserves trust and avoids confrontation.

goals.description: If negotiation fails, quietly extend my own share's completion time while drafting a polite, vague explanation for the delay to protect our dynamic.
goals.rationale: This is a backup plan driven by my fear of disappointing others. However, given my current energy levels (6) and the soft hardness of the tasks, I hope the first goal resolves it. If not, I will prioritize maintaining the relationship over perfecting the project scope right now.

desires: Keep the peace with teammate_A and avoid looking like I'm letting them down after they trusted me to take over.
